35.play: Was wrought by nature, not by vile offence,

36.play: I'll utter what my sorrows give me leave.

37.play: In Syracusa was I born, and wed

38.play: Unto a woman, happy but for me,

39.play: And by me, had not our hap been bad.

40.play: With her I lived in joy; our wealth increased

42.play: To Epidamnum; till my factor's death

43.play: And the great care of goods at random left

44.play: Drew me from kind embracements of my spouse:

45.play: From whom my absence was not six months old

46.play: Before herself, almost at fainting under

47.play: The pleasing punishment that women bear,

48.play: Had made provision for her following me

49.play: And soon and safe arrived where I was.

50.play: There had she not been long, but she became

51.play: A joyful mother of two goodly sons;

53.play: As could not be distinguish'd but by names.

54.play: That very hour, and in the self-same inn,

56.play: Of such a burden, male twins, both alike:

86.play: Fasten'd ourselves at either end the mast;

31.play: Bind Dromio too, and bear them to my house.

32.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: Run, master, run; for God's sake, take a house!

33.play: This is some priory. In, or we are spoil'd!

34.play: AEMELIA: Be quiet, people. Wherefore throng you hither?

35.play: ADRIANA: To fetch my poor distracted husband hence.

36.play: Let us come in, that we may bind him fast

37.play: And bear him home for his recovery.

38.play: ANGELO: I knew he was not in his perfect wits.

39.play: AEMELIA: How long hath this possession held the man?

40.play: ADRIANA: This week he hath been heavy, sour, sad,

41.play: And much different from the man he was;

42.play: But till this afternoon his passion

43.play: Ne'er brake into extremity of rage.

44.play: AEMELIA: Hath he not lost much wealth by wreck of sea?

45.play: Buried some dear friend? Hath not else his eye

46.play: Stray'd his affection in unlawful love?

47.play: A sin prevailing much in youthful men,

48.play: Who give their eyes the liberty of gazing.

49.play: Which of these sorrows is he subject to?

50.play: ADRIANA: To none of these, except it be the last;

51.play: Namely, some love that drew him oft from home.

52.play: AEMELIA: You should for that have reprehended him.

54.play: AEMELIA: Ay, but not rough enough.

55.play: ADRIANA: As roughly as my modesty would let me.

59.play: ADRIANA: It was the copy of our conference:

60.play: In bed he slept not for my urging it;

61.play: At board he fed not for my urging it;

62.play: Alone, it was the subject of my theme;

63.play: In company I often glanced it;

64.play: Still did I tell him it was vile and bad.

65.play: AEMELIA: And thereof came it that the man was mad.

66.play: The venom clamours of a jealous woman

67.play: Poisons more deadly than a mad dog's tooth.

68.play: It seems his sleeps were hinder'd by thy railing,

69.play: And therefore comes it that his head is light.

70.play: Thou say'st his meat was sauced with thy upbraidings:

72.play: Thereof the raging fire of fever bred;

73.play: And what's a fever but a fit of madness?

74.play: Thou say'st his sports were hinderd by thy brawls:

75.play: Sweet recreation barr'd, what doth ensue

77.play: Kinsman to grim and comfortless despair,

78.play: And at her heels a huge infectious troop

79.play: Of pale distemperatures and foes to life?

80.play: In food, in sport and life-preserving rest

81.play: To be disturb'd, would mad or man or beast:

82.play: The consequence is then thy jealous fits

83.play: Have scared thy husband from the use of wits.

84.play: LUCIANA: She never reprehended him but mildly,

85.play: When he demean'd himself rough, rude and wildly.

86.play: Why bear you these rebukes and answer not?

87.play: ADRIANA: She did betray me to my own reproof.

88.play: Good people enter and lay hold on him.

89.play: AEMELIA: No, not a creature enters in my house.

90.play: ADRIANA: Then let your servants bring my husband forth.

91.play: AEMELIA: Neither: he took this place for sanctuary,

92.play: And it shall privilege him from your hands

93.play: Till I have brought him to his wits again,

94.play: Or lose my labour in assaying it.

95.play: ADRIANA: I will attend my husband, be his nurse,

96.play: Diet his sickness, for it is my office,

97.play: And will have no attorney but myself;

98.play: And therefore let me have him home with me.

99.play: AEMELIA: Be patient; for I will not let him stir

100.play: Till I have used the approved means I have,

101.play: With wholesome syrups, drugs and holy prayers,

102.play: To make of him a formal man again:

103.play: It is a branch and parcel of mine oath,

104.play: A charitable duty of my order.

105.play: Therefore depart and leave him here with me.

106.play: ADRIANA: I will not hence and leave my husband here:

107.play: And ill it doth beseem your holiness

108.play: To separate the husband and the wife.

109.play: AEMELIA: Be quiet and depart: thou shalt not have him.

110.play: LUCIANA: Complain unto the duke of this indignity.

111.play: ADRIANA: Come, go: I will fall prostrate at his feet

112.play: And never rise until my tears and prayers

113.play: Have won his grace to come in person hither

114.play: And take perforce my husband from the abbess.

116.play: See where they come: we will behold his death.

117.play: LUCIANA: Kneel to the duke before he pass the abbey.

118.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Yet once again proclaim it publicly,

119.play: If any friend will pay the sum for him,

120.play: He shall not die; so much we tender him.

121.play: ADRIANA: Justice, most sacred duke, against the abbess!

122.play: DUKE SOLINUS: She is a virtuous and a reverend lady:

123.play: It cannot be that she hath done thee wrong.

124.play: ADRIANA: May it please your grace, Antipholus, my husband,

125.play: Whom I made lord of me and all I had,

126.play: At your important letters,--this ill day

127.play: A most outrageous fit of madness took him;

128.play: That desperately he hurried through the street,

129.play: With him his bondman, all as mad as he--

131.play: By rushing in their houses, bearing thence

132.play: Rings, jewels, any thing his rage did like.

133.play: Once did I get him bound and sent him home,

134.play: Whilst to take order for the wrongs I went,

135.play: That here and there his fury had committed.

136.play: Anon, I wot not by what strong escape,

137.play: He broke from those that had the guard of him;

138.play: And with his mad attendant and himself,

139.play: Each one with ireful passion, with drawn swords,

140.play: Met us again and madly bent on us,

141.play: Chased us away; till, raising of more aid,

142.play: We came again to bind them. Then they fled

143.play: Into this abbey, whither we pursued them:

144.play: And here the abbess shuts the gates on us

145.play: And will not suffer us to fetch him out,

146.play: Nor send him forth that we may bear him hence.

147.play: Therefore, most gracious duke, with thy command

148.play: Let him be brought forth and borne hence for help.

149.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Long since thy husband served me in my wars,

150.play: And I to thee engaged a prince's word,

151.play: When thou didst make him master of thy bed,

152.play: To do him all the grace and good I could.

153.play: Go, some of you, knock at the abbey-gate

154.play: And bid the lady abbess come to me.

155.play: I will determine this before I stir.

156.play: ADRIANA: Peace, fool! thy master and his man are here,

157.play: And that is false thou dost report to us.

158.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Come, stand by me; fear nothing. Guard with halberds!

159.play: ADRIANA: Ay me, it is my husband! Witness you,

160.play: That he is borne about invisible:

161.play: Even now we housed him in the abbey here;

162.play: And now he's there, past thought of human reason.

164.play: OF EPHESUS: Justice, most gracious duke, O, grant me justice!

165.play: Even for the service that long since I did thee,

166.play: When I bestrid thee in the wars and took

167.play: Deep scars to save thy life; even for the blood

168.play: That then I lost for thee, now grant me justice.

169.play: AEGEON: Unless the fear of death doth make me dote,

170.play: I see my son Antipholus and Dromio.

172.play: OF EPHESUS: Justice, sweet prince, against that woman there!

173.play: She whom thou gavest to me to be my wife,

174.play: That hath abused and dishonour'd me

175.play: Even in the strength and height of injury!

177.play: That she this day hath shameless thrown on me.

178.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Discover how, and thou shalt find me just.

180.play: OF EPHESUS: This day, great duke, she shut the doors upon me,

181.play: While she with harlots feasted in my house.

182.play: DUKE SOLINUS: A grievous fault! Say, woman, didst thou so?

183.play: ADRIANA: No, my good lord: myself, he and my sister

184.play: To-day did dine together. So befall my soul

185.play: As this is false he burdens me withal!

186.play: LUCIANA: Ne'er may I look on day, nor sleep on night,

187.play: But she tells to your highness simple truth!

188.play: ANGELO: O perjured woman! They are both forsworn:

189.play: In this the madman justly chargeth them.

191.play: OF EPHESUS: My liege, I am advised what I say,

192.play: Neither disturbed with the effect of wine,

193.play: Nor heady-rash, provoked with raging ire,

194.play: Albeit my wrongs might make one wiser mad.

195.play: This woman lock'd me out this day from dinner:

196.play: That goldsmith there, were he not pack'd with her,

197.play: Could witness it, for he was with me then;

198.play: Who parted with me to go fetch a chain,

199.play: Promising to bring it to the Porpentine,

200.play: Where Balthazar and I did dine together.

201.play: Our dinner done, and he not coming thither,

202.play: I went to seek him: in the street I met him

203.play: And in his company that gentleman.

204.play: There did this perjured goldsmith swear me down

205.play: That I this day of him received the chain,

206.play: Which, God he knows, I saw not: for the which

207.play: He did arrest me with an officer.

208.play: I did obey, and sent my peasant home

209.play: For certain ducats: he with none return'd

210.play: Then fairly I bespoke the officer

211.play: To go in person with me to my house.

213.play: My wife, her sister, and a rabble more

214.play: Of vile confederates. Along with them

215.play: They brought one Pinch, a hungry lean-faced villain,

217.play: A threadbare juggler and a fortune-teller,

219.play: A dead-looking man: this pernicious slave,

220.play: Forsooth, took on him as a conjurer,

221.play: And, gazing in mine eyes, feeling my pulse,

222.play: And with no face, as 'twere, outfacing me,

223.play: Cries out, I was possess'd. Then all together

224.play: They fell upon me, bound me, bore me thence

225.play: And in a dark and dankish vault at home

226.play: There left me and my man, both bound together;

227.play: Till, gnawing with my teeth my bonds in sunder,

228.play: I gain'd my freedom, and immediately

229.play: Ran hither to your grace; whom I beseech

231.play: For these deep shames and great indignities.

232.play: ANGELO: My lord, in truth, thus far I witness with him,

233.play: That he dined not at home, but was lock'd out.

234.play: DUKE SOLINUS: But had he such a chain of thee or no?

235.play: ANGELO: He had, my lord: and when he ran in here,

236.play: These people saw the chain about his neck.

237.play: OF EPHESUS: I never came within these abbey-walls,

238.play: Nor ever didst thou draw thy sword on me:

239.play: I never saw the chain, so help me Heaven!

240.play: And this is false you burden me withal.

241.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Why, what an intricate impeach is this!

242.play: I think you all have drunk of Circe's cup.

243.play: If here you housed him, here he would have been;

244.play: If he were mad, he would not plead so coldly:

245.play: You say he dined at home; the goldsmith here

246.play: Denies that saying. Sirrah, what say you?

247.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Sir, he dined with her there, at the Porpentine.

248.play: OF EPHESUS: 'Tis true, my liege; this ring I had of her.

249.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Saw'st thou him enter at the abbey here?

250.play: Why, this is strange. Go call the abbess hither.

251.play: I think you are all mated or stark mad.

252.play: AEGEON: Most mighty duke, vouchsafe me speak a word:

253.play: Haply I see a friend will save my life

254.play: And pay the sum that may deliver me.

255.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Speak freely, Syracusian, what thou wilt.

256.play: AEGEON: Is not your name, sir, call'd Antipholus?

257.play: And is not that your bondman, Dromio?

258.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Within this hour I was his bondman sir,

259.play: But he, I thank him, gnaw'd in two my cords:

260.play: Now am I Dromio and his man unbound.

261.play: AEGEON: I am sure you both of you remember me.

262.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Ourselves we do remember, sir, by you;

263.play: For lately we were bound, as you are now

264.play: You are not Pinch's patient, are you, sir?

265.play: AEGEON: Why look you strange on me? you know me well.

266.play: ANTIPHOLUS: I never saw you in my life till now.

267.play: AEGEON: O, grief hath changed me since you saw me last,

268.play: And careful hours with time's deformed hand

269.play: Have written strange defeatures in my face:

270.play: But tell me yet, dost thou not know my voice?

274.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: No, trust me, sir, nor I.

275.play: AEGEON: I am sure thou dost.

276.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Ay, sir, but I am sure I do not; and whatsoever a

277.play: man denies, you are now bound to believe him.

278.play: AEGEON: Not know my voice! O time's extremity,

279.play: Hast thou so crack'd and splitted my poor tongue

280.play: In seven short years, that here my only son

281.play: Knows not my feeble key of untuned cares?

282.play: Though now this grained face of mine be hid

284.play: And all the conduits of my blood froze up,

285.play: Yet hath my night of life some memory,

286.play: My wasting lamps some fading glimmer left,

287.play: My dull deaf ears a little use to hear:

288.play: All these old witnesses--I cannot err--

289.play: Tell me thou art my son Antipholus.

291.play: OF EPHESUS: I never saw my father in my life.

292.play: AEGEON: But seven years since, in Syracusa, boy,

293.play: Thou know'st we parted: but perhaps, my son,

294.play: Thou shamest to acknowledge me in misery.

296.play: OF EPHESUS: The duke and all that know me in the city

297.play: Can witness with me that it is not so

298.play: I ne'er saw Syracusa in my life.

299.play: DUKE SOLINUS: I tell thee, Syracusian, twenty years

300.play: Have I been patron to Antipholus,

301.play: During which time he ne'er saw Syracusa:

302.play: I see thy age and dangers make thee dote.

303.play: AEMELIA: Most mighty duke, behold a man much wrong'd.

304.play: ADRIANA: I see two husbands, or mine eyes deceive me.

305.play: DUKE SOLINUS: One of these men is Genius to the other;

306.play: And so of these. Which is the natural man,

307.play: And which the spirit? who deciphers them?

308.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: I, sir, am Dromio; command him away.

309.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: I, sir, am Dromio; pray, let me stay.

311.play: OF SYRACUSE: AEgeon art thou not? or else his ghost?

312.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: O, my old master! who hath bound him here?

313.play: AEMELIA: Whoever bound him, I will loose his bonds

314.play: And gain a husband by his liberty.

315.play: Speak, old AEgeon, if thou be'st the man

316.play: That hadst a wife once call'd AEmilia

317.play: That bore thee at a burden two fair sons:

318.play: O, if thou be'st the same AEgeon, speak,

319.play: And speak unto the same AEmilia!

320.play: AEGEON: If I dream not, thou art AEmilia:

321.play: If thou art she, tell me where is that son

322.play: That floated with thee on the fatal raft?

323.play: AEMELIA: By men of Epidamnum he and I

324.play: And the twin Dromio all were taken up;

325.play: But by and by rude fishermen of Corinth

326.play: By force took Dromio and my son from them

327.play: And me they left with those of Epidamnum.

328.play: What then became of them I cannot tell

329.play: I to this fortune that you see me in.

330.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Why, here begins his morning story right;

331.play: These two Antipholuses, these two so like,

332.play: And these two Dromios, one in semblance,--

333.play: Besides her urging of her wreck at sea,--

334.play: These are the parents to these children,

336.play: Antipholus, thou camest from Corinth first?

338.play: OF SYRACUSE: No, sir, not I; I came from Syracuse.

339.play: DUKE SOLINUS: Stay, stand apart; I know not which is which.

341.play: OF EPHESUS: I came from Corinth, my most gracious lord,--

342.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: And I with him.

344.play: OF EPHESUS: Brought to this town by that most famous warrior,

345.play: Duke Menaphon, your most renowned uncle.

346.play: ADRIANA: Which of you two did dine with me to-day?

349.play: ADRIANA: And are not you my husband?

351.play: OF EPHESUS: No; I say nay to that.

353.play: OF SYRACUSE: And so do I; yet did she call me so:

354.play: And this fair gentlewoman, her sister here,

357.play: I hope I shall have leisure to make good;

358.play: If this be not a dream I see and hear.

359.play: ANGELO: That is the chain, sir, which you had of me.

361.play: OF SYRACUSE: I think it be, sir; I deny it not.

363.play: OF EPHESUS: And you, sir, for this chain arrested me.

364.play: ANGELO: I think I did, sir; I deny it not.

365.play: ADRIANA: I sent you money, sir, to be your bail,

366.play: By Dromio; but I think he brought it not.

367.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: No, none by me.

369.play: OF SYRACUSE: This purse of ducats I received from you,

370.play: And Dromio, my man, did bring them me.

371.play: I see we still did meet each other's man,

372.play: And I was ta'en for him, and he for me,

373.play: And thereupon these errors are arose.

375.play: OF EPHESUS: These ducats pawn I for my father here.

376.play: DUKE SOLINUS: It shall not need; thy father hath his life.

377.play: OF EPHESUS: There, take it; and much thanks for my good cheer.

378.play: AEMELIA: Renowned duke, vouchsafe to take the pains

379.play: To go with us into the abbey here

380.play: And hear at large discoursed all our fortunes:

381.play: And all that are assembled in this place,

382.play: That by this sympathized one day's error

383.play: Have suffer'd wrong, go keep us company,

384.play: And we shall make full satisfaction.

385.play: Thirty-three years have I but gone in travail

386.play: Of you, my sons; and till this present hour

388.play: The duke, my husband and my children both,

389.play: And you the calendars of their nativity,

390.play: Go to a gossips' feast and go with me;

391.play: After so long grief, such festivity!

392.play: DUKE SOLINUS: With all my heart, I'll gossip at this feast.

393.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: Master, shall I fetch your stuff from shipboard?

395.play: OF EPHESUS: Dromio, what stuff of mine hast thou embark'd?

396.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: Your goods that lay at host, sir, in the Centaur.

398.play: OF SYRACUSE: He speaks to me. I am your master, Dromio:

399.play: Come, go with us; we'll look to that anon:

400.play: Embrace thy brother there; rejoice with him.

401.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: There is a fat friend at your master's house,

402.play: That kitchen'd me for you to-day at dinner:

403.play: She now shall be my sister, not my wife.

404.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Methinks you are my glass, and not my brother:

405.play: I see by you I am a sweet-faced youth.

406.play: Will you walk in to see their gossiping?

407.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: Not I, sir; you are my elder.

408.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: That's a question: how shall we try it?

409.play: DROMIO OF SYRACUSE: We'll draw cuts for the senior: till then lead thou first.

410.play: DROMIO OF EPHESUS: Nay, then, thus:

411.play: We came into the world like brother and brother;

412.play: And now let's go hand in hand, not one before another.
